pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to Doc4TF: what it is, why it exists, its key capabilities, and how the repository is organized. Links to all major child sections.

pages.title: Project Background and Goals
pages.purpose: Explains the motivation for Doc4TF (documentation rot, manual hyperlinking burden), its design philosophy (data-driven, ETL pipeline), and the target audience (Text-Fabric dataset authors and researchers).

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide for new users: prerequisites (Python 3.7+, Jupyter, Text-Fabric), downloading the notebook, configuring key variables, and running the pipeline to produce the first documentation set.

pages.title: Core Architecture
pages.purpose: Overview of the Doc4TF system architecture: the ETL pipeline, the central featureDict data structure, and the two main tools. Links to child pages for each major component.

pages.title: CreateFeatureDoc Notebook
pages.purpose: Deep dive into the primary notebook (CreateFeatureDoc.ipynb): configuration variables, the data ingestion phase, featureDict construction, sorting logic, cross-referencing/link generation, and file I/O helpers.

pages.title: Configuration and Output Formats
pages.purpose: Documents all user-facing configuration variables (resultLocation, typeOutput, tableLimit, customPageTitle, htmlStyle) and explains the Markdown vs. HTML output modes, including the handleSpace edge case.

pages.title: Feature Processing Pipeline
pages.purpose: Explains the internal processing functions: use(), processFeature(), getFeatureDescription(), setDataType(), the freqList frequency analysis pipeline, and the truncated-table mechanism.

pages.title: Version Delta Tool
pages.purpose: Covers the tools/determineDeltaBetweenVersions.ipynb notebook: its Load-Extract-Compare-Report lifecycle, the dual-API design, compare_feature_dicts logic, interval comparison, and the interactive HTML delta report.

pages.title: Generated Output
pages.purpose: Overview of the documentation artifacts produced by Doc4TF: the four index pages and the per-feature pages. Links to child pages describing the index structure and the sample results directory.

pages.title: Index Pages
pages.purpose: Describes the four generated index pages (featuresbynodetype.md, featuresbyname.md, featuresbydatatype.md, featuresbytype.md): their structure, anchor-based cross-linking, and how they are regenerated.

pages.title: Per-Feature Documentation Pages
pages.purpose: Explains the anatomy of an individual feature page (e.g., lemma.md, morph.md): header metadata table, description section, per-node-type frequency tables, truncation notes, and footer provenance line. Uses the results/ directory as the canonical example set.

pages.title: Documentation Website (GitHub Pages)
pages.purpose: Overview of the Jekyll-based GitHub Pages site under docs/: theme, configuration, layout, and asset pipeline. Links to child pages for configuration and front-end details.

pages.title: Jekyll Configuration
pages.purpose: Documents docs/_config.yml: site title, description, keywords, remote_theme (minimal@v0.2.0), plugins (jekyll-remote-theme, jekyll-seo-tag, jekyll-sitemap), and the show_downloads flag.

pages.title: Layout and Assets
pages.purpose: Covers docs/_layouts/default.html: the Liquid content injection point, navigation sidebar links (about, usage, legal, external paper, GitHub), SEO metadata, favicon assets, CSS/JS asset pipeline, and mobile scale-fix.

pages.title: Version History and Contributing
pages.purpose: Overview of the project's evolution, previous notebook versions, issue templates, and how to contribute. Links to child pages for version history and contribution guidelines.

pages.title: Notebook Version History
pages.purpose: Traces the evolution of CreateFeatureDoc across versions 0.2 through 0.5.1 (stored in previous_versions/), and the delta tool's older_versions/. Highlights key changes between releases.

pages.title: Contributing and Issue Reporting
pages.purpose: Explains how to report bugs and request features using the GitHub issue templates (.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/), and general contribution guidelines.

pages.title: Legal and Citation
pages.purpose: Covers the CC BY 4.0 license, attribution requirements, DOI/Zenodo citation, and the 'as-is' warranty disclaimer.

pages.title: License (CC BY 4.0)
pages.purpose: Full explanation of the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International license: permitted actions (copy, modify, redistribute, build applications), attribution requirements, no-additional-restrictions clause, and liability disclaimer.

pages.title: Citation and Academic Reference
pages.purpose: Documents how to cite Doc4TF in academic work using CITATION.cff, the Zenodo DOI (10.5281/zenodo.12705876), the associated Academia.edu paper, and the ORCID of the author.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: Definitions of all codebase-specific terms, Text-Fabric domain concepts, abbreviations, and jargon that an onboarding engineer needs to understand Doc4TF. Each entry includes code pointers.
